channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan Accountant Struggles to Save Despite Decent Income: Expert Advice
item.description: A 32-year-old Kenyan accountant, Muthoni Njakwe, is grappling with a common problem – earning a decent income (Sh62,000 monthly from a job and a side hustle) but failing to save anything. Her situatio...

item.title: Home Affairs clarifies validity of Zimbabwe Exemption Permits until 2027
item.description: The Department of Home Affairs has clarified that there has been no change in policy regarding Zimbabwe Exemption Permit (ZEP) holders, following confusion after Deputy Minister Njabulo Nzuza discusse...

item.title: Australia’s 2026 Defence Strategy overlooks modern realities and strategic challenges
item.description: Australia’s 2026 National Defence Strategy has drawn criticism for being a rehash of the 2024 version and failing to reflect the current global landscape. Although the government announced an addition...

item.title: Under a Bamboo Sky: Powerful documentary reveals Australian POWs' harrowing experiences in WWII
item.description: Under a Bamboo Sky is a compelling new Australian documentary that presents first-hand accounts from 63 Australian soldiers who endured unimaginable hardship as prisoners of war under the Japanese Imp...

item.title: Uhuru Torch Leader Pushes for Speedy Completion of Kigamboni Drainage Project
item.description: The National Uhuru Torch Race Leader for 2026, Mr. Wazo Mwang’onda, has urged the swift completion of the ongoing stormwater drainage construction along the Lingia–Nyakwale road in Kigamboni, Dar es S...

item.title: Rising fuel prices force Canadian craft brewers to shoulder higher shipping costs
item.description: Canadian craft brewers are grappling with rising transportation expenses as fuel prices surge across the country. With diesel and gasoline costs climbing despite temporary federal tax relief measures,...

item.title: IFC and Citigroup set up $98m local currency funding facility for South African businesses
item.description: The International Finance Corporation (IFC) together with Citigroup don launch one facility wey worth 1.6 billion rand to help South African businesses manage currency risk. Normally, many companies d...

item.title: Julius Malema sentenced to five years for firearm offences, appeal underway
item.description: EFF leader Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for unlawful possession of a firearm, along with additional sentences for related offences including unlawful possession of ammuniti...

item.title: Premier Allan faces calls to restore transparency in Victorian election funding
item.description: Campaign finance experts have called on the Victorian government to urgently recall parliament to reinstate rules on political donations following a High Court decision that invalidated all existing c...

item.title: India's Strategic Autonomy in the US-Israel-Iran Conflict
item.description: The article, likely discussing India's foreign policy stance amidst the escalating tensions between the US, Israel, and Iran, probably argues that India needs to maintain a degree of strategic indepen...

item.title: Vice President’s Office stresses Workers’ Council role in improving efficiency and accountability
item.description: Dodoma – The Minister of State in the Vice President’s Office, Engineer Hamad Yussuf Masauni, has emphasized that Workers’ Council meetings are not just formal events but essential platforms for boost...

item.title: Rapper ‘Hev Abi’ and Six Others Arrested in Quezon City for Illegal Firearms and Drugs
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Rapper 'Hev Abi' and six other individuals were arrested by authorities on Tuesday, April 14, in Diliman, Quezon City, following a search warrant executed for unlawful firearm po...

item.title: Machakos County rolls out training drive for 3,000 youths under empowerment scheme
item.description: The Machakos County Government has rolled out a new youth empowerment programme targeting 3,000 young people across the county. The initiative, unveiled by Governor Wavinya Ndeti at Nguluni Salvation ...

item.title: Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba Opens Lake Tanganyika Business and Investment Forum in Kigoma
item.description: Prime Minister Dr Mwigulu Nchemba has arrived in Kigoma Region on April 16, 2026, to officially open the Lake Tanganyika Business and Investment Forum. The event, which started on April 15 and will ru...

item.title: Helen Mirren's garden and its influence on household food expenses
item.description: The article explores how Helen Mirren's gardening choices could have implications for the cost of food. While the full content of the article was not accessible, it likely discusses how homegrown prod...

item.title: Alberta company challenges CFIA suspension over freeze-dried breast milk service
item.description: Booby Food, a small Lethbridge-based business founded by Janna Hattingh, has been shut down by the Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) after five years of operating a unique service that freeze-dri...

item.title: State Governments to Drive Nigeria’s Next Phase of Economic Growth
item.description: For years, Nigerians dey look Abuja for every major economic reform, from naira devaluation to subsidy removal. But now, di real work wey go turn macro stability into everyday prosperity dey happen fo...

item.title: Trump's Public Disagreement with Pope Leo Draws Criticism from African Catholics
item.description: African Catholics are expressing strong disapproval of US President Donald Trump’s recent public criticism of Pope Leo, the first US-born leader of the Catholic Church. The fallout began with Trump’s ...

item.title: Marcos shows physical fitness amid public health rumors
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. showcased his physical fitness by doing jumping jacks, jogging, and lifting a 10-kilogram sack of rice during a public event in Manila on April 16, 2026. The demonstrati...

item.title: UK economy experiences growth, but challenges remain
item.description: The UK economy has shown signs of returning to growth after a period of stagnation and economic uncertainty. While this may seem like positive news, economists caution that the growth could be short-l...
